title: consumer credit / rejection rates / lending standards | Data Release Other | Consumers Report Higher Credit Rejection Rates, Expect Fewer...
summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York's Survey of Consumer Expectations Credit Access Survey for October 2024 shows that reported rejection rates for credit applications increased across all categories in 2024, with mortgage refinances (25.6%) and auto loans (11.4%) reaching new series highs since the module began in 2013. The overall rejection rate rose to 21.0% from 20.1% in 2023, while lender-initiated account closures hit a new series high of 7.4%.
